dx_split_squats_anteromedial_femoral_con.label: Split Squats - anteromedial knee pain (loaded LE)

recommendations.title: Knee outward alignment
recommendations.description: Dynamic valgus: slightly turn your toes and knee outward and pretend you're pushing your knee against a wall. (While in position, slightly turn the toes and knee of your front foot slightly outward. Keep them turned outward at this angle throughout the entire movement, try not to let your knee collapse inward towards midline.)

steps.title: Setup
steps.text: While in position, slightly turn the toes and knee of your front foot slightly outward.

steps.title: The Movement
steps.text: Keep them turned outward at this angle throughout the entire movement, try not to let your knee collapse inward towards midline.

recommendations.why_it_works: This modification addresses dynamic valgus by adjusting your movement pattern to reduce stress on the affected area.

recommendations.title: Keep knees behind toes
recommendations.description:
Anterior knee translation: Sink the back knee straight down to the ground without pushing your hips forward, dont let your knee move past your toes, or move the front foot slightly forward. (While in position, keep your back leg perpendicular to the ground by sinking your knee downward in a straight line without pushing your hips forward.
Pay attention to your front knee so that it does not move forward past your toes, by keeping your shin vertical/perpendicular to the ground.
If you notice your knee on the front leg moving beyond your toes as you drop down after making adjustments, you may need to move the front foot slightly forward.)

steps.title: Setup
steps.text: While in position, keep your back leg perpendicular to the ground by sinking your knee downward in a straight line without pushing your hips forward.

steps.title: The Movement
steps.text: Pay attention to your front knee so that it does not move forward past your toes, by keeping your shin vertical/perpendicular to the ground.

steps.title: Check Yourself
steps.text: If you notice your knee on the front leg moving beyond your toes as you drop down after making adjustments, you may need to move the front foot slightly forward.

recommendations.why_it_works: This modification addresses anterior knee translation by adjusting your movement pattern to reduce stress on the affected area.

recommendations.title: Hip hinge forward lean
recommendations.description: Decrease quad activation: focusing on hinging at the hips, lean yout trunk forwrd slightly. *always cue for pelvic tilt/core engagement* (While in position, engage your core by tilting/rotating your pelvis backward (pull your belly button towards your spine), then slightly lean your trunk forward by hinging at your hips (don’t round your back))
